qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in Scadenzario print template via id_anagrafica GET parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the exact same vulnerable code location (templates/scadenzario/init.php line 46), same vulnerable parameter (id_anagrafica), same injection sink (direct string concatenation into SQL query via str_replace), and same root cause (missing use of prepare() function for input sanitization) as described in the advisory GHSA-q6g3-fv43-m2w6.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference in file viewing endpoint allows unauthorized file access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes an IDOR vulnerability in view.php for unauthorized file access, which is a different vulnerability type (IDOR vs SQL injection), different vulnerable code location (view.php vs templates/scadenzario/init.php), and different root cause (missing authorization check vs missing SQL sanitization) than the advisory-described SQL injection in the Scadenzario print template.

selected_finding.title: SQL injection in Scadenzario print template via id_anagrafica GET parameter

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The sqli_scadenzario_print_template finding correctly identifies the exact vulnerability described in advisory GHSA-q6g3-fv43-m2w6: SQL injection in templates/scadenzario/init.php line 46 via the id_anagrafica GET parameter. The finding demonstrates the same vulnerable code pattern (direct string concatenation of user input into SQL query without proper sanitization) at the same location. However, the PoC execution failed due to Docker network timeout when attempting to pull base images, preventing runtime verification of exploit success or bug activation. The finding is classified as Tier3 because: (1) it matches the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause, (2) it is a qualifying_vuln, (3) PoC execution was attempted but failed due to environment/network issues before the vulnerable logic could be activated.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-q6g3-fv43-m2w6 identifies SQL injection in templates/scadenzario/init.php line 46 via id_anagrafica parameter
Finding sqli_scadenzario_print_template identifies the same vulnerable code location: templates/scadenzario/init.php:46
Finding demonstrates the same root cause: get('id_anagrafica') is concatenated directly into SQL query via str_replace without using prepare() function
Finding shows the same vulnerable pattern: $module_query = str_replace('1=1', '1=1 AND `co_scadenziario`.`idanagrafica`="'.get('id_anagrafica').'"', $module_query);
Finding correctly identifies the attack path: id_anagrafica parameter -> Filter::get() -> HTMLPurifier sanitization (no SQL protection) -> direct concatenation into SQL WHERE clause
PoC reproduction bundle exists but execution failed due to Docker build network timeout
blockers_to_higher_tier: Docker build failed with network timeout when pulling php:8.3-apache-bullseye and composer:2 base images
Unable to verify exploit_success_predicate or bug_activation_predicate through runtime execution
Cannot confirm SLEEP(3) time-based blind SQL injection or UNION SELECT data exfiltration without successful PoC run

poc_executions.observed_effect: Docker build failed during metadata fetch for base images (php:8.3-apache-bullseye and composer:2) due to network timeout when connecting to docker.io registry. The build process failed at the image resolution stage and never reached the vulnerable code execution phase.
